helicopter__inverted_autorotation.text:
# Inverted Autorotation

## What This Maneuver Is
You roll the helicopter upside-down, then cut collective to *negative flat pitch*, letting the rotor windmill and maintain RPM through descent ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” all while inverted. You recover by re-engaging negative collective and pulling out.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain inverts every control input. Cyclic directions reverse completely. "Pulling collective" now means pushing the stick down. RPM management works backward ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” you're adding negative pitch to maintain head speed, not positive. Two mental inversions happening simultaneously under time pressure.

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (30+ seconds, consistent)
- Normal autorotation entries and recoveries ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” automatic, no thinking
- Inverted circuits with confidence
- Understand negative pitch range on your collective (typically -12 to -14 degrees)

## The Stick Movements

**Collective (left stick vertical):** Push DOWN to increase negative pitch ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” this is your "throttle hold" equivalent inverted. Too little negative pitch = RPM bleeds. Too much = RPM overspeeds.

**Cyclic (right stick):** ALL directions are reversed. Push right, helicopter rolls left. Push forward, tail goes up (nose drops toward ground). Think "opposite everything."

**Tail rotor (left stick horizontal):** Still corrects yaw, but torque direction is reversed ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” anticipate opposite pedal inputs.

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” Inverted Hover to Throttle Cut**
Goal: Feel RPM decay inverted. Enter stable inverted hover at 6m, cut throttle, immediately add negative collective. Hold 3 seconds, re-engage.
*Mistake:* Freezing on collective. *Fix:* Pre-set your hand position before cutting.
*Advance when:* RPM stays in band consistently.

**Phase 2 ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” Controlled Inverted Descent**
Goal: Maintain altitude loss rate of ~1m/sec. Modulate negative collective to manage RPM ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” treat it like a normal auto but push instead of pull.
*Mistake:* Over-pitching, RPM spikes. *Fix:* Smaller collective inputs, feel the rotor.
*Advance when:* 10-second descents with stable RPM.

**Phase 3 ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” Cyclic Control During Descent**
Goal: Maintain heading and attitude while descending. Add small cyclic corrections.
*Mistake:* Overcorrecting because brain defaults to upright logic. *Fix:* Verbalize "opposite" before each correction.
*Advance when:* Heading holds within 15 degrees.

**Phase 4 ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” Entry From Forward Flight**
Goal: Inverted forward pass ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚В†ГѓВ‚Г‚В’ roll inverted ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚В†ГѓВ‚Г‚В’ throttle cut ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚В†ГѓВ‚Г‚В’ autorotation.
*Mistake:* Rolling inverted too slowly, losing energy. *Fix:* Commit to the roll aggressively.
*Advance when:* Entry is consistent, no scrambling.

**Phase 5 ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” Full Recovery**
Goal: Flare inverted (push cyclic forward = nose pitches toward ground/bleeds speed), then roll upright and catch with positive collective.
*Mistake:* Rolling upright too late with no RPM left. *Fix:* Begin recovery at 4m minimum.
*Advance when:* Recovery is smooth, no RPM collapse.

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Consistently losing tail during descent
- RPM drops below 80% before you react
- Cyclic reversals still require conscious thought
- Recovery altitude keeps shrinking dangerously

## Sim Tips
1. Slow sim speed to 70% until Phase 3
2. Display RPM gauge prominently on screen
3. Practice inverted hover with eyes closed briefly ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” builds feel over sight
4. Repeat Phase 5 recoveries 20x in one session ГѓВѓГ‚ВўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚В” muscle memory only

helicopter__pirouette_to_inverted.text:
# Pirouette to Inverted

## What This Maneuver Is
A continuous yaw rotation (pirouette) that transitions seamlessly through upright into **fully inverted flight**, maintaining altitude and position throughout.

---

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain must flip its entire control mapping mid-rotation. Collective that saves you upright **kills you inverted**. Aileron and elevator reverse. You must anticipate the inversion point *before* it arrives and pre-load negative collective ГўВЂВ” all while the helicopter is spinning.

---

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (60+ seconds, no drama)
- Clean stationary pirouettes both directions
- Inverted forward/backward/sideways flight
- Basic piro-flips at reduced speed

---

## The Stick Movements

| Control | What It Does |
|--------|--------------|
| **Rudder** | Drives continuous yaw ГўВЂВ” keep it constant, don't stab |
| **Collective** | Transitions from positive ГўВ†В’ through zero ГўВ†В’ negative as nose crosses inverted |
| **Aileron/Elevator** | Constantly corrected as orientation changes ГўВЂВ” inputs *reverse* when inverted |
| **Rate** | Slow pirouette (Г‚Вј speed) initially ГўВЂВ” speed is the enemy while learning |

---

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГўВЂВ” Find the Inversion Point**
Slow pirouette, stop exactly inverted. Hold 5 seconds. Repeat until stopping precisely inverted is automatic.
*Mistake:* Overshooting past inverted. *Fix:* Anticipate 15Г‚В° early.
*Advance when:* You stop inverted 8/10 attempts, no altitude loss.

**Phase 2 ГўВЂВ” Collective Transition Practice**
From upright hover, manually roll inverted (no pirouette). Practice smoothly swapping from positive to negative collective.
*Mistake:* Pause at zero collective ГўВЂВ” you'll drop. *Fix:* Treat it like a door, not a stop sign. Keep moving through it.
*Advance when:* Transition takes under 1 second, altitude stays within 3 feet.

**Phase 3 ГўВЂВ” Slow Pirouette Through Inverted**
Г‚Вј throttle pirouette. Cross through inverted, maintain 2 seconds, come back upright.
*Mistake:* Collective swap happens too late. *Fix:* Start going negative at **90Г‚В° before** full inverted.
*Advance when:* Clean, no altitude spike, no panic inputs.

**Phase 4 ГўВЂВ” Full 360Г‚В° Pirouette**
One complete rotation maintaining constant altitude throughout.
*Mistake:* Slowing the pirouette rate when inverted. *Fix:* Lock that rudder input ГўВЂВ” consistent speed builds consistent muscle memory.
*Advance when:* 5 clean rotations in a row.

**Phase 5 ГўВЂВ” Speed and Precision**
Increase pirouette rate. Add forward/backward positioning corrections while spinning.
*Mistake:* Correcting position with wrong aileron direction. *Fix:* Talk yourself through it: "inverted, inputs reversed."
*Advance when:* Looks boring to watch.

---

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Altitude spikes or drops **every** time you cross inverted
- You hesitate on collective at the inversion point
- Inverted hover degrades under 30 seconds when nervous
- You're mentally "guessing" aileron direction past 180Г‚В°

---

## Sim Tips
1. **Enable slow-motion** ГўВЂВ” watch exactly where collective changes
2. **Map a kill switch** ГўВЂВ” reset fast, remove fear
3. **Practice inverted hover first thing every session** ГўВЂВ” keep it sharp
4. **Record replays** ГўВЂВ” your hands feel smooth; the replay shows the truth

helicopter__backwards_flight.text:
# Backwards Flight ГўВЂВ” RC Helicopter

## What This Maneuver Is
Flying the helicopter tail-first toward you in a controlled, straight path ГўВЂВ” cyclic, tail, and throttle working together while your orientation is completely mirrored.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain still maps "back cyclic = helicopter moves back." But the helicopter's nose is pointing away, so **left is right, right is left** on aileron. Elevator stays logical; aileron is fully reversed. Split-second correction instincts will kill you here.

## Before You Start
- Rock-solid nose-in hovering
- Confident pirouette corrections
- Figure-8s in all orientations
- Tail-in and side-in circuits completed cleanly

## The Stick Movements
| Control | What it does in backwards flight |
|---|---|
| **Collective** | Same ГўВЂВ” altitude control, no change |
| **Tail rotor** | Same direction feel ГўВЂВ” left stick left/right still yaws normally |
| **Back cyclic (elevator)** | Pushes helicopter **toward you** ГўВЂВ” this feels natural |
| **Left aileron** | Moves helicopter **right** ГўВЂВ” completely backwards |
| **Right aileron** | Moves helicopter **left** ГўВЂВ” completely backwards |

**Golden rule:** Elevator is your friend. Aileron is the enemy until rewired.

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГўВЂВ” Static Backwards Hover**
Hover tail-in 10 feet out, 5 feet high. Hold position 60 seconds. Don't move laterally. *Mistake: Overcorrecting aileron. Fix: Smaller inputs, pause between corrections.* Advance when stable for 2 minutes.

**Phase 2 ГўВЂВ” Backwards Hover Lateral Shuffles**
Intentionally drift left/right 3 feet and correct back. Burn in reversed aileron reflex deliberately. *Mistake: Panic reverting to forward-flight instincts. Fix: Verbalize "opposite" aloud.* Advance when 10 corrections feel automatic.

**Phase 3 ГўВЂВ” Slow Backwards Tracking**
Pull back cyclic gently, fly 20 feet toward yourself, stop. Maintain heading ГўВЂВ” no weathervaning. *Mistake: Tail drifting sideways during translation. Fix: Lead with tiny tail rotor corrections.* Advance when 5 clean passes completed.

**Phase 4 ГўВЂВ” Backwards Rectangle**
Fly backward, 90Г‚В° yaw, fly backward again ГўВЂВ” four sides. Keep altitude constant. *Mistake: Skidding the corners. Fix: Stop forward motion before yawing.* Advance when rectangle looks square, not rhombus-shaped.

**Phase 5 ГўВЂВ” Backwards Figure-8**
Two backward circles joined. Aileron reversals happen constantly here. Speed controlled, altitude locked. *Mistake: Rushing the connecting point. Fix: Treat each loop separately.* Advance when completed 3 times without correction bailout.

**Phase 6 ГўВЂВ” Speed and Distance**
Push speed up. Double the distance. Add light wind conditions in sim. This is mission-ready backwards flight.

## Warning Signs ГўВЂВ” Stop and Rebuild
- Consistently correcting the wrong aileron direction
- Altitude surging during directional inputs
- Losing heading within 5 seconds of backwards translation
- Requiring full-deflection corrections to recover

## Sim Tips
1. Set a **visual marker** on screen ГўВЂВ” fly backward toward it, not randomly
2. Use **slow-motion replay** ГўВЂВ” watch your stick inputs vs helicopter response
3. Practice Phase 1 **eyes closed briefly** to feel trim, not see it
4. Map a **panic button** to instantly pause ГўВЂВ” analyze before resetting

helicopter__rolling_circle.text:
# Rolling Circle вЂ” RC Helicopter

## What This Maneuver Is
Fly a perfect horizontal circle while simultaneously rolling the helicopter continuously around its longitudinal axis. The nose traces the circle; the fuselage spins like a barrel roll the entire time.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain must decouple two simultaneous tasks: maintaining circular flight geometry AND managing a constantly-changing roll orientation. Every 90В° of roll changes which stick input creates lift versus drift. Cyclic inputs rotate with the helicopter вЂ” what was "up elevator" is now "left aileron" halfway through the roll. You're essentially re-mapping your inputs 360В° continuously.

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (30+ seconds, hands relaxed)
- Clean barrel rolls with altitude recovery
- Consistent nose-in circle at speed
- Comfortable pirouetting funnels

## The Stick Movements

**Aileron (roll):** Constant input вЂ” steady left or right roll rate throughout. This is your metronome.

**Elevator:** Rotates with the helicopter. When upright = forward push for circle. When inverted = it reverses вЂ” you push the same direction but the helicopter's reference has flipped. Think "always push toward the circle center" relative to the canopy.

**Collective:** Constantly pumping. Upright = normal positive pitch. Inverted = negative pitch to maintain altitude. Transitions happen twice per rotation at the knife-edge points.

**Rudder:** Keeps the nose tracking the circle. Minor corrections only вЂ” if you're fighting rudder, your roll rate or circle speed is wrong.

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 вЂ” Slow Roll, Large Circle**
Goal: Establish timing. Fly a wide circle, add slow aileron roll. Focus purely on collective transitions at inverted. *Mistake: Late collective input в†’ altitude loss. Fix: Anticipate inverted before you're there.* Advance when altitude stays within 10 feet.

**Phase 2 вЂ” Elevator Mapping**
Goal: Add active elevator to maintain circle geometry. Watch for the circle drifting outward when inverted. *Mistake: Releasing elevator when inverted. Fix: Remind yourself "push stays push."* Advance when circle shape holds through full rotations.

**Phase 3 вЂ” Tighten the Circle**
Goal: Reduce diameter by 30%. More coordinated elevator and collective needed. *Mistake: Over-correcting with rudder. Fix: Slow your roll rate instead.* Advance when 5 consecutive clean rotations.

**Phase 4 вЂ” Speed Up the Roll**
Goal: Double roll rate. Collective pumping must accelerate. *Mistake: Flat spots in the roll. Fix: Commit вЂ” no hesitation at knife-edge.* Advance when roll rate feels automatic.

**Phase 5 вЂ” Presentation Quality**
Goal: Consistent altitude, round circle, smooth roll rate. No visible corrections. Advance when judges can't see your mistakes.

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Altitude drops every inverted pass
- Circle becomes an oval when inverted
- Rudder constantly sawing back and forth
- Roll rate changes mid-maneuver

## Sim Tips
- Fly directly in front of you only вЂ” eliminate perspective confusion early
- Record and replay every attempt вЂ” watch altitude trace
- Deliberately pause at inverted hover mid-practice to rebuild the inverted elevator map
- Practice slow-roll funnels first as a warm-up every session

helicopter__elevator.text:
# Elevator (RC Helicopter)

## What This Maneuver Is
A pure forward-backward pitching rotation around the helicopter's lateral axis вЂ” nose pitches up and down continuously while maintaining a fixed point in space, or flown as a pendulum arc. Think of it as looping through vertical pitch cycles without rolling or yawing.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain wants to add collective on the upswing and reduce it on the downswing вЂ” that's helicopter logic. Here, you must *resist* that instinct. Tail rotor authority changes dramatically as orientation shifts. Gyroscopic precession fights your inputs during rapid pitch reversals.

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (10+ seconds stable)
- Comfortable nose-in pirouette corrections
- Clean tic-tocs both axes
- Can recover from unexpected inverted attitudes reflexively

## The Stick Movements

**Cyclic (Right Stick):** Drives the pitch rotation. Forward cyclic pitches nose down, back cyclic pitches nose up. You're pumping this rhythmically вЂ” the timing IS the maneuver.

**Collective (Left Stick vertical):** Must counteract altitude loss. Add collective as the disc unloads during rotation. You'll need more collective than feels natural during the upward-pitching phase.

**Tail Rotor (Left Stick horizontal):** Constant micro-corrections. Gyroscopic precession will yaw the nose as pitch rate changes вЂ” anticipate it, don't chase it.

**Throttle:** Maintain head speed. Let the governor work; don't manually compensate.

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 вЂ” Slow Pitch Rocks**
Goal: Feel the collective demand change. Pitch 30В° nose-up, hold, return. 10 reps. *Mistake:* No collective compensation в†’ drops altitude. *Fix:* Add 15% collective at full pitch. *Advance:* Altitude stays within 1 meter.

**Phase 2 вЂ” Full Nose-Up to Inverted Arc**
Goal: Pitch to 90В° bank then 180В° (inverted). Hold inverted 2 seconds, recover. *Mistake:* Panicking and pulling collective inverted в†’ climbs violently. *Fix:* Reduce collective when inverted. *Advance:* Inverted hold is stable.

**Phase 3 вЂ” Continuous Slow Elevator**
Goal: Full 360В° pitch loop at slow speed, maintaining position. *Mistake:* Drifting laterally вЂ” you're adding roll without knowing it. *Fix:* Video yourself, isolate the drift axis. *Advance:* 5 consecutive loops, same spot.

**Phase 4 вЂ” Speed Up the Rotation**
Goal: Increase rotation rate to 1 loop per 3 seconds. *Mistake:* Losing tail at the top. *Fix:* Pre-load opposite rudder entering the top. *Advance:* 10 consecutive clean loops.

**Phase 5 вЂ” Precision and Combination**
Goal: Defined entry/exit points, fixed altitude, combine with lateral drift. Fly elevator while translating sideways.

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Consistent altitude loss through the top of the loop
- Yaw drift exceeds 15В° per loop
- Recovery from botched inverted takes more than 1 second
- Collective inputs are reactive, not anticipatory

## Sim Tips
1. Slow motion first вЂ” dial down simulation speed to 50%
2. Enable telemetry overlays showing pitch angle numerically
3. Practice inverted hover 10 minutes before every elevator session
4. Use a grid backdrop to spot lateral drift immediately
